name: Eunice Newton Foote
tagline: First to Discover the Greenhouse Effect
taglineCn: 第一个发现温室效应的人
bio: Eunice Newton Foote was the first scientist to demonstrate the greenhouse effect of carbon dioxide. In 1856 — three years before John Tyndall's more famous experiments — she showed that a cylinder of CO2 heated more in sunlight than one filled with air, and that it cooled more slowly. As a woman, she was not allowed to present her own paper at the AAAS meeting; a male colleague read it for her. Her work was forgotten for over 150 years.
bioCn: 尤尼斯·牛顿·富特是第一位证明二氧化碳温室效应的科学家。1856年——比约翰·廷德尔更著名的实验早三年——她表明装有CO2的圆筒在阳光下比装满空气的加热更多，冷却更慢。作为一名女性，她不被允许在AAAS会议上宣读自己的论文；由一位男同事代为宣读。她的工作被遗忘了150多年。
achievements: First to demonstrate CO2 greenhouse effect (1856)
Preceded Tyndall's experiments by three years
Work rediscovered after 150+ years
achievementsCn: 第一个证明CO2温室效应（1856年）
比廷德尔的实验早三年
工作在150多年后被重新发现
references: Foote, E.N. 'Circumstances Affecting the Heat of the Sun's Rays,' American Journal of Science, 1856.

name: Inge Lehmann
bio: Inge Lehmann was a Danish seismologist who discovered that Earth has a solid inner core within its liquid outer core. By analyzing seismic waves from earthquakes, she proposed in 1936 that the Earth's core is not a single molten sphere, as previously believed, but has a solid inner part. She lived to 104, making her one of the longest-lived scientists in history, and continued publishing research into her 70s.
bioCn: 英格·莱曼是一位丹麦地震学家，她发现地球在液态外核内有一个固态内核。通过分析地震产生的地震波，她于1936年提出地球的核心不像之前认为的那样是单一的熔融球体，而是有一个固态的内部。她活到了104岁，成为历史上最长寿的科学家之一，70多岁仍在发表研究。
achievements: Discovered Earth's solid inner core (1936)
Lived to age 104
Fundamentally changed understanding of Earth's structure
achievementsCn: 发现地球固态内核（1936年）
活到104岁
从根本上改变了对地球结构的理解
references: Lehmann, I. 'P',' Publications du Bureau Central Séismologique International, 1936.

name: Marie Tharp
bio: Marie Tharp created the first comprehensive map of the ocean floor, revealing the Mid-Atlantic Ridge and providing crucial evidence for the theory of plate tectonics and continental drift. Banned from research ships because she was a woman, she worked from shore analyzing sonar data. When she first identified the rift valley, her colleague Bruce Heezen dismissed her findings as 'girl talk.' Her maps fundamentally changed our understanding of Earth's geology.
bioCn: 玛丽·萨普创建了第一张综合性海底地图，揭示了大西洋中脊，为板块构造和大陆漂移理论提供了关键证据。因为她是女性而被禁止上研究船，她在岸上工作分析声纳数据。当她首次发现裂谷时，她的同事布鲁斯·黑曾将她的发现斥为'女人话'。她的地图从根本上改变了我们对地球地质的理解。
achievements: Created first comprehensive map of the ocean floor
Discovered the Mid-Atlantic Ridge rift valley
Provided key evidence for plate tectonics theory
achievementsCn: 创建第一张综合性海底地图
发现大西洋中脊裂谷
为板块构造理论提供关键证据
references: Felt, Hali. Soundings: The Story of the Remarkable Woman Who Mapped the Ocean Floor. Henry Holt, 2012.

name: Wangari Maathai
tagline: Nobel Peace Prize for Environmental Conservation
taglineCn: 因环境保护获诺贝尔和平奖
bio: Wangari Maathai was the first African woman to receive the Nobel Peace Prize (2004) for her contribution to sustainable development, democracy, and peace through her Green Belt Movement. She mobilized rural women across Kenya to plant over 51 million trees, combating deforestation and providing livelihoods. She was also the first woman in East and Central Africa to earn a PhD and the first female professor in Kenya.
bioCn: 旺加里·马塔伊是第一位获得诺贝尔和平奖（2004年）的非洲女性，表彰她通过绿带运动对可持续发展、民主和和平的贡献。她动员肯尼亚各地的农村妇女种植了超过5100万棵树，对抗森林砍伐并提供生计。她还是东非和中非第一位获得博士学位的女性，也是肯尼亚第一位女性教授。
achievements: Nobel Peace Prize (2004) for environmental conservation
Green Belt Movement planted 51+ million trees
First African woman to receive the Nobel Peace Prize
achievementsCn: 因环境保护获诺贝尔和平奖（2004年）
绿带运动种植超过5100万棵树
第一位获诺贝尔和平奖的非洲女性
quote: It's the little things citizens do. That's what will make the difference. My little thing is planting trees.
quoteCn: 正是公民做的小事会带来改变。我的小事就是种树。
references: Maathai, W. Unbowed: A Memoir. Knopf, 2006.

name: Sylvia Earle
tagline: Her Deepness — Queen of the Deep
bio: Sylvia Earle is a marine biologist, explorer, and author who has been at the forefront of ocean exploration for over five decades. In 1979, she walked untethered on the sea floor at 381 meters depth — deeper than any human before. Known as 'Her Deepness,' she was the first female chief scientist of NOAA and has led over 100 expeditions. She founded Mission Blue, a global initiative to establish marine protected areas.
bioCn: 西尔维亚·厄尔是一位海洋生物学家、探险家和作家，五十多年来一直处于海洋探索的最前沿。1979年，她在381米深处无系绳行走于海底——比之前任何人都深。被称为'深海女王'，她是NOAA第一位女性首席科学家，领导了100多次探险。她创立了Mission Blue，一个建立海洋保护区的全球倡议。
achievements: Set solo ocean depth record at 381 meters
First female chief scientist of NOAA
Led over 100 ocean expeditions
achievementsCn: 创下381米独自海底深潜纪录
NOAA第一位女性首席科学家
领导100多次海洋探险
quote: No water, no life. No blue, no green.
quoteCn: 没有水就没有生命。没有蓝色就没有绿色。
references: Earle, Sylvia. The World Is Blue. National Geographic, 2009.

name: Katia Krafft
bio: Katia Krafft was a French volcanologist who, together with her husband Maurice, documented volcanic eruptions around the world with extraordinary courage and scientific rigor. Their footage and photographs of eruptions from dangerously close range have been invaluable for volcanology and public safety. Their film of pyroclastic flows directly influenced evacuation procedures that saved tens of thousands of lives during the 1991 Mount Pinatubo eruption. Tragically, both Kraffts died in a pyroclastic flow at Mount Unzen, Japan, in 1991.
bioCn: 卡提亚·克拉夫特是一位法国火山学家，与丈夫莫里斯一起以非凡的勇气和科学严谨性记录了世界各地的火山喷发。他们在危险近距离拍摄的火山喷发影像和照片对火山学和公共安全具有无价的价值。他们拍摄的火山碎屑流影片直接影响了1991年皮纳图博火山喷发时的疏散程序，拯救了数万人的生命。不幸的是，两位克拉夫特于1991年在日本云仙岳的一次火山碎屑流中遇难。
achievements: Pioneered close-range volcanic documentation
Footage saved tens of thousands during Pinatubo eruption
Documented over 150 volcanic eruptions worldwide
achievementsCn: 开创近距离火山记录
影像在皮纳图博喷发期间拯救了数万人
在全球记录了150多次火山喷发

name: Mary Leakey
taglineCn: 证明人类直立行走的古人类学先驱
bio: Mary Leakey was one of the most significant paleoanthropologists of the 20th century. In 1959, she discovered the Zinjanthropus skull (Paranthropus boisei) at Olduvai Gorge in Tanzania, reshaping understanding of human evolution. Her most celebrated discovery came in 1978 when she found the 3.6-million-year-old Laetoli footprints, providing definitive evidence that early hominids walked upright. Her meticulous excavation techniques set the standard for paleoanthropological fieldwork.
bioCn: 玛丽·利基是20世纪最重要的古人类学家之一。1959年，她在坦桑尼亚奥杜瓦伊峡谷发现了东非人头骨（鲍氏傍人），重塑了人们对人类进化的认识。她最著名的发现是1978年发掘的距今360万年的莱托利脚印，为早期人类直立行走提供了确凿证据。她严谨细致的发掘技术为该领域树立了标准。
achievements: Discovered Laetoli footprints proving bipedalism (1978)
Found Zinjanthropus skull at Olduvai Gorge (1959)
Set standards for paleoanthropological excavation techniques
achievementsCn: 发现证明直立行走的莱托利脚印（1978年）
在奥杜瓦伊峡谷发现东非人头骨（1959年）
为古人类学发掘技术树立标准
references: Leakey, M. Disclosing the Past. Doubleday, 1984.

name: Tanya Atwater
bio: Tanya Atwater is a geophysicist who made groundbreaking contributions to the theory of plate tectonics. Her seminal 1970 paper explained the tectonic history of western North America, including the formation of the San Andreas Fault, fundamentally changing our understanding of the region's geology. She created widely used educational animations illustrating plate tectonic evolution over millions of years. As a professor at UC Santa Barbara, she has been a leading educator and researcher in geophysics.
bioCn: 坦尼娅·阿特沃特是一位地球物理学家，对板块构造理论做出了开创性贡献。她1970年发表的里程碑式论文解释了北美洲西部的构造历史，包括圣安德烈亚斯断层的形成，从根本上改变了人们对该地区地质的理解。她制作了广泛使用的教育动画，展示了数百万年来板块构造的演变。作为加州大学圣巴巴拉分校的教授，她一直是地球物理学领域的杰出教育者和研究者。
achievements: Published landmark 1970 paper on western North American tectonics
Created influential plate tectonic educational animations
Leading geophysics researcher at UC Santa Barbara
achievementsCn: 发表1970年关于北美西部构造的里程碑式论文
制作有影响力的板块构造教育动画
加州大学圣巴巴拉分校杰出地球物理学研究者
references: Atwater, T. 'Implications of Plate Tectonics for the Cenozoic Tectonic Evolution of Western North America,' GSA Bulletin, 1970.

name: Katharine Hayhoe
taglineCn: 顶尖气候科学家和科学传播者
bio: Katharine Hayhoe is a leading climate scientist and one of the most effective science communicators of her generation. As chief scientist at The Nature Conservancy, her research focuses on understanding the regional impacts of climate change and helping cities and communities prepare for climate risks. She was named one of TIME's 100 Most Influential People in 2014 for her efforts to bridge the gap between scientific evidence and public understanding. She is particularly known for engaging faith communities in conversations about climate stewardship.
bioCn: 凯瑟琳·海霍是一位顶尖气候科学家，也是她这一代最有影响力的科学传播者之一。作为大自然保护协会的首席科学家，她的研究聚焦于理解气候变化的区域影响，帮助城市和社区应对气候风险。2014年，她因致力于弥合科学证据与公众认知之间的鸿沟而入选《时代》杂志100位最具影响力人物。她尤其以在信仰社区中推动气候保护对话而闻名。
achievements: Chief scientist at The Nature Conservancy
TIME 100 Most Influential People (2014)
Pioneer in bridging climate science and faith communities
achievementsCn: 大自然保护协会首席科学家
《时代》杂志100位最具影响力人物（2014年）
在气候科学与信仰社区间架起桥梁的先驱
references: Hayhoe, K. Saving Us: A Climate Scientist's Case for Hope and Healing in a Divided World. One Signal Publishers, 2021.

name: Katsuko Saruhashi
bio: Katsuko Saruhashi was a pioneering Japanese geochemist who developed the first method to accurately measure carbon dioxide levels in seawater. Her research traced radioactive fallout from nuclear weapons tests across the Pacific Ocean, providing crucial scientific evidence that supported nuclear test ban treaties. She was the first woman elected to the Science Council of Japan. To encourage women in science, she established the Saruhashi Prize, Japan's most prestigious award for women scientists.
bioCn: 猿桥胜子是一位开创性的日本地球化学家，开发了第一种精确测量海水中二氧化碳含量的方法。她的研究追踪了核武器试验产生的放射性沉降物在太平洋的扩散，为支持核试验禁令条约提供了关键科学证据。她是第一位当选日本学术会议成员的女性。为了鼓励女性从事科学研究，她设立了猿桥奖——日本最具声望的女科学家奖项。
achievements: Developed first method to measure CO2 in seawater
Research supported nuclear test ban treaties
First woman elected to Science Council of Japan
achievementsCn: 开发第一种测量海水CO2的方法
研究支持了核试验禁令条约
第一位当选日本学术会议成员的女性
references: Saruhashi Prize for Women Scientists — https://www.saruhashi.net/

name: Joanne Simpson
tagline: First American Woman PhD in Meteorology
taglineCn: 美国第一位女性气象学博士
bio: Joanne Simpson was the first woman in the United States to earn a PhD in meteorology, receiving her doctorate from the University of Chicago in 1949. She conducted pioneering research on tropical clouds and hurricanes, proposing the influential 'hot tower' hypothesis that explained how tropical cumulonimbus clouds drive global atmospheric circulation. At NASA, she led the development of the Tropical Rainfall Measuring Mission (TRMM) satellite, which revolutionized our understanding of tropical rainfall and its role in global climate.
bioCn: 乔安妮·辛普森是美国第一位获得气象学博士学位的女性，1949年从芝加哥大学获得博士学位。她对热带云系和飓风进行了开创性研究，提出了具有深远影响的'热塔'假说，解释了热带积雨云如何驱动全球大气环流。在NASA，她领导开发了热带降雨测量任务（TRMM）卫星，彻底改变了我们对热带降雨及其在全球气候中作用的理解。
achievements: First woman in the US to earn a PhD in meteorology (1949)
Proposed the influential 'hot tower' hypothesis
Led development of NASA's TRMM satellite
achievementsCn: 美国第一位获得气象学博士学位的女性（1949年）
提出有影响力的'热塔'假说
领导开发NASA的TRMM卫星
references: NASA Goddard Space Flight Center: Joanne Simpson tribute.
